pr.title: Add __mem matching C, optimization flag for current matching, remove line ending in ssbm.sha1
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Replaced the hand-written assembly for the early .init memory routines with matching C for memset and __fill_mem, added the new source/header files, adjusted the linker script so __mem.o(.text) is placed at the start of .init at 0x80003100, and changed compiler flags from -O2,p to -O4,p because -O2,p did not match due to variable ordering in memset. The Makefile also gained an INCLUDES variable covering include and src, and ssbm.sha1 was normalized by removing a problematic trailing carriage return/no-newline issue.

key_files.role: New matching C implementation for memset and __fill_mem, replacing the removed assembly block from asm/init.s.
New header declaring memset and __fill_mem using unsigned long for the size/count parameter.
Removed the assembly implementation of memset and func_80003130-sized fill routine from the .init section.
Explicitly inserts __mem.o(.text) into the .init section before remaining *(.init) input sections.
Adds INCLUDES := -i include -i src and changes CFLAGS optimization from -O2,p to -O4,p.
Normalized checksum file line ending/no-newline state so sha1sum works for the author.

what_changed: Added src/__mem.c with C versions of memset and __fill_mem. memset calls __fill_mem(dst, val, n) and then returns the original dst pointer.
Added src/__mem.h with include guard ___mem_ and prototypes for void *memset(void *dst, int val, unsigned long n) and void __fill_mem(void *dst, int val, unsigned long n).
Deleted the assembly implementation of memset and the following fill routine from asm/init.s, leaving later init symbols such as func_800031F4 in place.
Updated ldscript.lcf so .init BIND(0x80003100) begins with __mem.o(.text) followed by *(.init), preserving the expected address placement for the decompiled memory routines.
Changed Makefile CFLAGS from -O2,p -i include to -O4,p $(INCLUDES), with INCLUDES defined as -i include -i src.
Adjusted ssbm.sha1 to remove a trailing carriage return or line ending issue described by the PR body as breaking sha1sum for the author.
smart_moves: Kept the linker ordering explicit by placing __mem.o(.text) before *(.init), which is important when replacing assembly located at fixed early .init addresses.
Captured a matching-sensitive compiler option change in the PR body: -O2,p was non-matching due to variable ordering for the return in memset, while -O4,p matched the current implementation.
Moved the routine into src while adding -i src to the compiler include paths, avoiding ad hoc include paths in individual files.

The C implementation mirrors the assembly structure with byte alignment handling, replicated byte value construction, 32-byte unrolled word stores, remaining word stores, and tail byte stores, which makes it easier to match generated code against the original assembly.
Preserved memset semantics by saving the original dst through the wrapper and returning it after __fill_mem mutates its local dst pointer during filling.
decomp_lessons: For early runtime/library routines, matching C may need both source replacement and linker-script placement; adding the C file alone is not enough if the original code lived in .init at fixed addresses.
Optimization flags can be function-critical. In this PR, -O2,p produced non-matching variable ordering for memset, while -O4,p was used for the current match.
When decompiling low-level memory routines, pointer pre-decrement and pre-increment idioms can reproduce assembly patterns such as stbu/stwu update stores.
Use explicit object placement in ldscript.lcf when the decompiled object must occupy a specific section position previously held by assembly.
Repository checksum files are sensitive to line endings; a stray carriage return or unexpected newline state can break sha1sum even when the hash text appears unchanged.
naming_conventions: The new source/header pair uses the runtime-style name __mem.c and __mem.h.
The helper routine is named __fill_mem, while the public C library entry point remains memset.
The header guard added here is ___mem_, matching the double-underscore file name but using a lower-case guard. No review feedback is available to confirm whether this became a preferred convention.
Function parameters in the new code use short names dst, val, and n, matching typical C library memory routine naming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace assembly in asm/init.s only after adding matching C and forcing object placement in .init through ldscript.lcf.
Use -O4,p for this matching C case; the PR body states -O2,p caused non-matching variable ordering for the return in memset.
Model the fill helper with unsigned long temporaries and casts through unsigned char * and unsigned long * to trigger the byte and word store patterns seen in the removed assembly.
Replicate byte value expansion with v |= v << 24 | v << 16 | v << 8 when v is nonzero, corresponding to building a repeated-byte word for word stores.
Use loops that decrement with while (--i) / while (--n) and pre-incremented stores to resemble the original counted loops and update-address stores.
review_feedback: No inline review comments or issue comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The available review evidence is limited to two reviews with no excerpted comments, so reviewer rationale cannot be reconstructed from this slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify __mem.c or __mem.h to see whether this initial matching implementation or naming was refined.
Search for additional Makefile optimization flag changes after PR #6 to determine whether -O4,p remained global or became per-file.
Look for linker script changes involving .init and __mem.o to understand how early runtime object placement evolved.
Search for comments or later issues mentioning ssbm.sha1 line endings or sha1sum portability.
